rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Realign Defendant-Heirs As Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 2, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' and Petitioners' motion to realign defendant-heirs Bruce Felker, Russ Felker and Kahleb Felker as plaintiffs is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Southwire Company, Llc'S Motion For Stay Based Upon Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 2, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. The following tentative ruling was posted on March 30, 2015 and there was no notice given to the Court that any part was contesting it, therefore the Court will adopt its tentative ruling: Defendant Alcatel Lucent USA, Inc.'s joinder in Southwire Company, LLC's motion to stay on grounds of forum non conveniens is off calendar as moot because the underlying motion remains on calendar and will be ruled upon. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY HIGHTOWER ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Southwire Company, Llc For Stay Based Upon Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 2, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. The following tentative ruling was posted on March 30, 2015 and there was no notice given to the Court that any part was contesting it, therefore the Court will adopt its tentative ruling: Defendant Southwire Company, LLC's motion to stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation remain in California. Although not required when seeking solely a stay rather than a dismissal, the Court will point out that defendant did not demonstrate that Georgia is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
